# 🧪 Liquid Glass UI

An ultra-premium, high-fidelity glassmorphic component library and CSS framework. It utilizes advanced **SVG displacement distortion maps**, multi-layered **specular reflection shading**, and **organic mercury fluid morphing animations** to create stunning, interactive, and futuristic user interfaces.

---

## ✨ Features

* **Molten Vector Physics:** Sliding active panels and pills stretch and merge fluidly like molten glass or mercury.
* **3D Specular Tilt:** Specular reflection flares track cursor coordinates to simulate realistic physical refraction.
* **Highly Customizable:** Fully driven by standard CSS Custom Properties (blur, scale, opacity, color saturation, accents).
* **Cross-Framework Compatible:** Standalone library support for **React**, **Vite**, **Next.js (App Router & SSR)**, and **Vanilla HTML/CSS**.
* **Zero Dependencies:** Fully optimized for lightweight performance and compiled ESM / UMD distributions.

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

### 1. Installation

Install the package via npm (when published):

```bash
npm install liquid-glass-ui
```

*Or manually copy the files directly into your project's components/styles directory.*

### 2. General Setup (Importing Styles)

Import the core layout styles in your application's entry file (e.g., `main.jsx`, `App.tsx`, or `_app.js`):

```javascript
import 'liquid-glass-ui/dist/style.css';
```

---

## ⚛️ Usage in React & Vite

Wrap your root layout with the `<LiquidGlassProvider>` to automatically inject the distortion vectors, then use container components to build layouts:

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { LiquidGlassProvider, LiquidGlassContainer } from 'liquid-glass-ui';

export default function App() {
  return (
    <LiquidGlassProvider 
      blur={14} 
      scale={0.5} 
      saturation={150} 
      tint="#bbbbbc"
      accent="#fc3c44"
    >
      <div style={{ padding: '40px', background: '#09090b', minHeight: '100vh' }}>
        
        {/* Basic Frosted Glass Panel */}
        <LiquidGlassContainer className="p-6 rounded-2xl max-w-sm">
          <h3 className="text-white text-lg font-bold">Frosted Panel</h3>
          <p className="text-white/60 text-xs mt-2">Ultra premium liquid borders.</p>
        </LiquidGlassContainer>
        
        {/* Interactive 3D Card with dynamic cursor specular shine */}
        <LiquidGlassContainer 
          className="p-6 rounded-2xl max-w-sm mt-6"
          enableSpecularHover={true}
        >
          <h3 className="text-white text-lg font-bold">Interactive Card</h3>
          <p className="text-white/60 text-xs mt-2">Hover to slide glass highlights.</p>
        </LiquidGlassContainer>

      </div>
    </LiquidGlassProvider>
  );
}
```

---

## ⚡ Usage in Next.js (App Router / SSR)

To integrate seamlessly with Next.js Server Components, ensure the Provider is designated as a Client Component, or wrap your layout nodes inside the exported provider directly.

Create a provider wrapper file: `components/GlassProvider.jsx`:

```jsx
"use client";

import React from 'react';
import { LiquidGlassProvider } from 'liquid-glass-ui';

export default function GlassProvider({ children }) {
  return (
    <LiquidGlassProvider blur={12} scale={0.5}>
      {children}
    </LiquidGlassProvider>
  );
}
```

Then, wrap your layout root `app/layout.jsx`:

```jsx
import './globals.css';
import 'liquid-glass-ui/dist/style.css';
import GlassProvider from '@/components/GlassProvider';

export const metadata = {
  title: 'Next.js Liquid Glass App',
};

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en">
      <body>
        <GlassProvider>
          {children}
        </GlassProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

---

## 🌐 Usage in Vanilla HTML & CSS

For single page sites or vanilla configurations, you can copy the raw CSS variables and SVG vector node definition straight into your document.

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <style>
    :root {
      --lg-blur: 12px;
      --lg-scale: 0.5;
      --lg-saturation: 150%;
      --lg-opacity: 0.12;
      --lg-glass-tint: #bbbbbc;
      --lg-light: #ffffff;
      --lg-dark: #000000;
      --lg-reflex-light: 0.3;
      --lg-reflex-dark: 2;
    }

    /* Core frosted class mapping */
    .glass-panel {
      position: relative;
      background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-glass-tint) calc(var(--lg-opacity) * 100%), transparent);
      backdrop-filter: blur(var(--lg-blur)) url(#liquid-glass-filter) saturate(var(--lg-saturation));
      -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(var(--lg-blur)) saturate(var(--lg-saturation));
      
      /* High-fidelity 10-layer physical glass specular shadow */
      box-shadow: 
        inset 0 0 0 1px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-light) calc(var(--lg-reflex-light) * 15%), transparent),
        inset 1.8px 3px 0px -2px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-light) calc(var(--lg-reflex-light) * 90%), transparent), 
        inset -2px -2px 0px -2px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-light) calc(var(--lg-reflex-light) * 80%), transparent), 
        inset -3px -8px 1px -6px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-light) calc(var(--lg-reflex-light) * 60%), transparent), 
        inset -0.3px -1px 4px 0px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-dark) calc(var(--lg-reflex-dark) * 12%), transparent), 
        inset -1.5px 2.5px 0px -2px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-dark) calc(var(--lg-reflex-dark) * 20%), transparent), 
        inset 0px 3px 4px -2px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-dark) calc(var(--lg-reflex-dark) * 20%), transparent), 
        inset 2px -6.5px 1px -4px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-dark) calc(var(--lg-reflex-dark) * 10%), transparent), 
        0px 1px 5px 0px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-dark) calc(var(--lg-reflex-dark) * 10%), transparent), 
        0px 6px 16px 0px color-mix(in srgb, var(--lg-dark) calc(var(--lg-reflex-dark) * 12%), transparent);
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body style="background: #030303;">

  <div class="glass-panel" style="width: 300px; height: 180px; border-radius: 24px; padding: 24px; margin: 40px auto; color: white;">
    <h3>Vanilla Glass</h3>
    <p style="opacity: 0.6; font-size: 13px;">No dependencies fluid glassmorphism.</p>
  </div>

  <!-- SVG Displacement Filter Map -->
  <svg aria-hidden="true" style="position: absolute; width: 0; height: 0; pointer-events: none;">
    <defs>
      <filter id="liquid-glass-filter" primitiveUnits="objectBoundingBox">
        <feImage result="map" width="100%" height="100%" x="0" y="0" href="data:image/webp;base64,,,,," />
      </filter>
    </defs>
  </svg>

</body>
</html>
```

---

## 🛠️ Building & Bundling

To bundle the showcase site or rebuild the package distribution files:

### Build the Interactive Demo Site
```bash
npm run build
```
*Outputs static files to `dist/` ready to be uploaded to GitHub Pages, Netlify, or Vercel.*

### Build the Component Library Bundles
```bash
npm run build:lib
```
*Outputs publish-ready `index.js`, `index.esm.js`, and `style.css` modules to `dist/` using Vite Library Mode.*

---

## 📦 Publishing to npm / GitHub Packages

1. Make sure your library is built successfully:
   ```bash
   npm run build:lib
   ```
2. Log in to your npm registry:
   ```bash
   npm login
   ```
3. Publish the library with public access:
   ```bash
   npm publish --access public
   ```

---
